items.name: Lore
items.source_story_title: Lore – Open source version control system designed for scalability
items.summary: Open-source version control built for game assets and Unreal workflows.
items.why_included: Commenters called it a promising challenger to Perforce for game development.
items.evidence: Top comments said the announcement looked very promising for Unreal game dev and that Perforce needs a challenger.

items.name: Browser Use
items.source_story_title: How we run Firecracker VMs inside EC2 and start browsers in less than 1s
items.summary: Browser automation platform for AI agents.
items.why_included: A commenter said they would rather use Browser Use if it supported Android browsers.
items.evidence: One comment explicitly preferred Browser Use, but wanted Android-browser support before switching.

items.name: ApiFlash
items.source_story_title: How we run Firecracker VMs inside EC2 and start browsers in less than 1s
items.summary: Website screenshot API built on Chrome and AWS Lambda.
items.why_included: A commenter described ApiFlash as the screenshot API they already run in production.
items.evidence: The thread included a commenter saying they run a screenshot API with Chromium in an AWS Lambda container image instead of Firecracker.

items.name: MicroUI
items.source_story_title: MicroUI – A tiny, portable, immediate-mode UI library written in ANSI C
items.summary: Tiny portable immediate-mode UI library in ANSI C.
items.why_included: Commenters praised it as minimal, practical, and easy to drop into toy projects.
items.evidence: Top comments said it was interesting in how minimal it is and that it was a go-to for personal toy projects.

items.name: Ribbie
items.source_story_title: Show HN: An 8-bit live gamecast for baseball
items.summary: 8-bit live baseball gamecast with animated play-by-play.
items.why_included: The show thread drew strong praise and comparison to earlier baseball-following tools.
items.evidence: Comments called it adorable and super cool, and one commenter said they had used playball before and looked forward to updates.

items.name: Inkwash
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Inkwash, a watercolor sketching app and explanation
items.summary: Watercolor sketching app with an explanatory physics demo.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the wash and compared it to other watercolor tools.
items.evidence: A commenter said the wash was gorgeous, while another asked whether the author had tried Rebelle.

items.name: Immich
items.source_story_title: Want your images back? That'll be $5
items.summary: Self-hosted photo backup and management app.
items.why_included: A commenter strongly recommended it as the open photo stack they want instead of vendor lock-in.
items.evidence: One commenter called Immich good, free/open, and said it avoided being an admin-for-life project.

items.name: Rebelle
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Inkwash, a watercolor sketching app and explanation
items.summary: Digital watercolor painting software with a realistic online experiment.
items.why_included: The Inkwash thread surfaced Rebelle as the obvious comparison point for watercolor simulation.
items.evidence: A commenter asked if the author had tried Rebelle and pointed to its online watercolor/brush experiment.

items.name: Bubbles
items.source_story_title: Hacker News but for independent blogs
items.summary: Independent-blog front page with voting, comments, and curated briefings.
items.why_included: Commenters repeatedly praised its calmer, more human feel versus social-media doomscrolling.
items.evidence: People said Bubbles felt refreshing, diverse, humane, and more curated than a firehose.
